cells.text: Graffiti on a freight train car
Military vehicle (in use or decommissioned)
A neon sign (anything clearly in the neon style counts even if it's not true neon)
Sporting event center for any professional or college sport/team
Any US state flag
A sign with a date on it
A sign indicating something was established on a date in or before 1950 (the thing doesn't have to still be extant or functional)

cells.category: Easy license plate letter/number game, only one per card from this category
cells.text: A license plate serial containing the numeral 1
A damaged sign
Any bus stop or bus station (signed school bus stops count)
Any depiction of or a live bald eagle
Any wildlife/animal warning sign
any photo of your choice
A picture taken while clearly riding public transit of any kind (train/metro/subway/light rail, bus, etc.)
Any depiction of, or a live example of, a state bird in its state (e.g. Northern cardinal in Illinois; list)
Private property/no trespassing sign
A sign with a population count on it
A dirty vehicle where someone has drawn/written in its dirt
Advertisement for a local radio station
A vehicle with three or more bumper stickers
A sign marking a municipality border

Any US or Canada license plate that is not a "state standard" design (for ease of identification, "state standard" defined as the designs that are on the front page here, even though there are others that are considered "standard")
A motel with exterior room entrances
A roundabout/rotary
Any county building
